notes.n-m7:
Today I worked through the Antichrist profile with focus on His Profile section and a deep dive into Daniel 11:37 — "The Desire of Women."

I started by tracking down the Greek word *apoleia* (G684) using e-Sword's right-click Greek search function. This revealed that "son of perdition" in John 17:12 and "son of destruction" in 2 Thessalonians 2:3 both use the same Greek root, even though English translates them differently. Perdition means final, irreversible destruction — the Antichrist is marked for eternal ruin from the beginning, just as Judas was.

I worked through the four kingdoms (Babylon, Medo-Persia, Greece, Rome) and confirmed that Daniel 2:37–38 explicitly identifies Nebuchadnezzar as the head of gold — the first kingdom. From there, the historical progression is clear: each subsequent kingdom conquered the one before it. The Antichrist rises from this revived Roman system.

On Daniel 11:37, I pulled the Hebrew literals using e-Sword: *chemdah* (desire, delight, precious) and *nashiym* (women, plural). Looking at the verse structure, "desire of women" sits sandwiched between two religious rejections — gods of his fathers and any other god — making it clearly part of a list of religious denials, not personal behavior.

I evaluated all four interpretations: Tammuz (fertility deity women worshiped), Messianic Hope (the Messiah women longed to bear), Homosexuality (weakest grammatically and contextually), and my own reading from April 13th — Cold Disregard for Human Longing. The fourth resonates most with me: the Antichrist's total self-exaltation makes him indifferent to what humanity values. He already believes he *is* God, so human longing is irrelevant to him. This detachment is what makes him dangerous — he has no conscience, no pressure point, no human feeling to exploit.
